subject: [PATCH] mm: migrate: requeue destination folio on deferred split queue

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David (Arm) questioned whether re-queuing large folios after migration would reintroduce them to the deferred split list, even if they had previously been removed.
sentiment_signals: requested clarification on potential lock ordering issue
reviews.raw_body:
Doesn't that mean that you will readd any large folios, even if already
previously taken off the list after scanning?

So I am not sure if your "if the source was a large folio that was on
the queue." comment is accurate?

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the source folio needs to be checked for being on the deferred split queue before migration, and agreed to add a new flag (PAGE_WAS_ON_DEFERRED_SPLIT) to record this state. The destination folio will then be requeued if the source was on the queue.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes you are right. How about something like below? We also won't need to check
for anon and non-device folios with this as we only set the the flag if it was
already on deferred_split list.


diff --git a/mm/migrate.c b/mm/migrate.c
index ece77ccb2ec0..9e0780d380e4 100644
--- a/mm/migrate.c
+++ b/mm/migrate.c
@@ -1137,7 +1137,9 @@ static int move_to_new_folio(struct folio *dst, struct folio *src,
 enum {
        PAGE_WAS_MAPPED = BIT(0),
        PAGE_WAS_MLOCKED = BIT(1),
-       PAGE_OLD_STATES = PAGE_WAS_MAPPED | PAGE_WAS_MLOCKED,
+       PAGE_WAS_ON_DEFERRED_SPLIT = BIT(2),
+       PAGE_OLD_STATES = PAGE_WAS_MAPPED | PAGE_WAS_MLOCKED |
+                         PAGE_WAS_ON_DEFERRED_SPLIT,
 };
 
 static void __migrate_folio_record(struct folio *dst,
@@ -1373,6 +1375,15 @@ static int migrate_folio_move(free_folio_t put_new_folio, unsigned long private,
                goto out_unlock_both;
        }
 
+       /*
+        * Record whether the source folio is on the deferred split queue
+        * before move_to_new_folio(), which unqueues it via
+        * __folio_migrate_mapping().
+        */
+       if (folio_test_large(src) && folio_test_large_rmappable(src) &&
+           !data_race(list_empty(&src->_deferred_list)))
+               old_page_state |= PAGE_WAS_ON_DEFERRED_SPLIT;
+
        rc = move_to_new_folio(dst, src, mode);
        if (rc)
                goto out;
@@ -1393,6 +1404,15 @@ static int migrate_folio_move(free_folio_t put_new_folio, unsigned long private,
        if (old_page_state & PAGE_WAS_MAPPED)
                remove_migration_ptes(src, dst, 0);
 
+       /*
+        * Requeue the destination folio on the deferred split queue if
+        * the source was on the queue.  The source is unqueued in
+        * __folio_migrate_mapping(), so we record and check the state
+        * from before move_to_new_folio().
+        */
+       if (old_page_state & PAGE_WAS_ON_DEFERRED_SPLIT)
+               deferred_split_folio(dst, false);
+
 out_unlock_both:
        folio_unlock(dst);
        folio_set_owner_migrate_reason(dst, reason);

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Zi Yan noted that migrate_pages() tries to split partially mapped folios before migration, leaving only underutilized THPs in the deferred_list. They suggested either scanning for underused pages before migration or detecting zero pages at page copy time and splitting after migration.

Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where the destination folio is already on the deferred split queue, and suggested adding a check to avoid re-queuing it in this scenario.
reviews.raw_body:
BTW, migrate_pages() tries to split partially mapped folios before migration[1],
so what remains in the deferred_list would be:

1. partially mapped but with a pin,
2. fully mapped but potentially underused.

I wonder if you want to do an underused scan before migration and try to split
underused THPs. Or to avoid this additional scan, find a way of detecting
zero pages at page copy time and split it after migration.

Anyway, it seems that all large folios are in this deferred_list. Maybe, like
David suggested in his LSFMM proposal, we should scan large folios on LRU lists
at reclaim time instead, since there is not much difference between deferred_list
and LRU lists right now.


[1] https://elixir.bootlin.com/linux/v6.19.3/source/mm/migrate.c#L1840

---

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

reviews.author: Matthew Wilcox
reviews.summary:
reviewer questioned the necessity of using data_race() in conjunction with list_empty(), pointing out that the latter already contains a READ_ONCE() to ensure atomicity

Reviewer Matthew Wilcox questioned the necessity of the patch's comment, suggesting it is redundant due to well-chosen variable names.
reviews.raw_body:
Why do you need data_race() here?  list_empty() contains a READ_ONCE()
so shouldn't be necessary?

---

You've done a great job of the naming.  So much so that the comment
seems entirely unnecessary?

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author pushed back on feedback to requeue destination folio, arguing that scanning THPs for zeros has a cost and they should be kept as is unless there is memory pressure.

Author responded to Zi Yan's feedback by explaining that not requeueing the destination folio on deferred split queue may be beneficial in certain scenarios, specifically when additional memory is not needed after migration.

Author acknowledged a concern about storing information in page flags and agreed to modify the patch by keeping it as a local variable instead.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the patch does not acquire split_queue_lock before accessing, similar to folio_unqueue_deferred_split(), and did not indicate a plan to fix this

Author acknowledges that the patch can be simplified, agrees to restructure it in a future version (v2), and plans to reduce comment verbosity.
reviews.raw_body:
Ah mainly because we dont acquire split_queue_lock before accessing,
similar to what we do in folio_unqueue_deferred_split().

---

Yeah there is a much simpler version in reply to Zi's review in [1].
I found the whole migrate_folio_move() function quite verbose already,
was keeping up with the theme of that function lol. I will see if I can
cut down on the comment in [1] as well. Thanks!

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/all/28e48b47-f215-4e4a-b55a-01dbf293ff35@linux.dev/

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch is LGTM and thanks the submitter for improvements
reviews.raw_body:
LGTM. Thanks for improving it.

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

2026-03-06.patch_summary: This patch fixes a problem where underutilized THPs (Transparent Huge Pages) would be lost after NUMA migration due to the destination folio not being requeued on the deferred split queue. The patch adds a call to `deferred_split_folio()` for large rmappable folios in the `migrate_folio_move()` function, ensuring that these folios are properly handled during migration.
